findings.summary: sklearn referenced in health_check optional probe but never declared in any dependency group
evidence: Line 193: optional_deps = ['ollama', 'scikit-learn', 'reportlab'] — scikit-learn is probed at runtime
pyproject.toml has no scikit-learn in any dependency-group
The probe is benign (import-try), but its presence implies callers might expect availability documentation
findings.suggestion: Either add scikit-learn to a named optional dep-group with a comment, or remove it from the runtime probe list if it is not used anywhere

findings.summary: projects/code_project/scripts/optimization_analysis.py is 1654 LOC, zero importers, and has no test coverage
evidence: existing_findings: 'Untested critical module (1654 LOC, 0 importers) — high blast radius'
existing_findings: 'orphaned: Orphaned file (1654 LOC): zero importers, not an entry point'
Contains 2x sys.path mutation at import time, 2x monster functions (>150 LOC)
findings.suggestion: Extract domain logic into projects/code_project/src/ so it can be unit-tested, reducing the script to a thin orchestrator per the stated architecture contract

findings.summary: scripts/01_run_tests.py is 623 LOC with zero importers and no test coverage despite being core pipeline infrastructure
evidence: existing_findings: 'Untested critical module (623 LOC, 0 importers) — high blast radius'
existing_findings: 'orphaned: Orphaned file (623 LOC): zero importers, not an entry point'
Module imports from infrastructure but is itself never imported or tested
findings.suggestion: Extract the logic-heavy portions (coverage threshold checks, test suite configuration building) into infrastructure/reporting/ so they can be tested independently; the script itself can remain thin

findings.summary: integrity.py has Args/Returns docstrings on functions whose signatures are already self-documenting
evidence: verify_file_integrity: docstring Args says 'file_paths: List of file paths to verify' — identical to parameter name and type hint
verify_cross_references: 'markdown_files: List of markdown files to check' — restates the parameter name
7 such Args/Returns blocks found in this single file where type hints make the prose redundant
findings.suggestion: Keep the one-line summary only; drop Args/Returns sections when the type annotations and parameter names are already unambiguous

findings.summary: logger.info calls on entry to data-transformation functions add noise without operational value
evidence: integrity.py line 55: logger.info(f'Verifying integrity of {len(file_paths)} files') — fires on every call including test calls
integrity.py line 95: logger.info(f'Verifying cross-references in {len(markdown_files)} files')
integrity.py line 168: logger.info(f'Verifying data consistency for {len(data_files)} files')
These are internal library functions, not CLI entry points; info-level entry logging is appropriate only at boundaries
findings.suggestion: Downgrade these entry-log calls to logger.debug(); reserve logger.info for I/O completion confirmations at the top-level boundary

findings.summary: infrastructure/reporting/test_runner.py belongs conceptually in infrastructure/core/ not reporting/
evidence: test_runner.py provides pytest subprocess execution and retry/coverage loop logic
It is imported by scripts/01_run_tests.py — an orchestrator script, not a reporting consumer
The reporting/ directory profile shows this file is zoned 'test' by the analysis tool, indicating the name misleads the scanner
All other files in reporting/ generate output artifacts (JSON, HTML, markdown, dashboards); test_runner.py drives process execution
findings.suggestion: Move test_runner.py to infrastructure/core/ (alongside pipeline.py and checkpoint.py) where process-execution utilities live, or create infrastructure/testing/ if the surface grows

findings.summary: infrastructure/core/__init__.py docstring references infrastructure.core.performance but that module does not exist
evidence: Line 10: 'infrastructure.core.performance   — CodeProfiler, ProfilingMetrics, etc.'
Glob of infrastructure/core/*.py shows no performance.py
The actual performance monitoring is in infrastructure/core/function_profiler.py
findings.suggestion: Update the docstring reference from 'infrastructure.core.performance' to 'infrastructure.core.function_profiler'

findings.summary: The logging concern is split across 5 files in infrastructure/core/ creating fragmented navigation
evidence: logging_utils.py is the hub (135 importers) and re-imports from the 4 satellite files
None of the satellite files have independent importers — they exist only to be consumed by logging_utils.py
This split adds 4 files of indirection without enabling independent reuse or reducing coupling
findings.suggestion: Collapse logging_constants, logging_formatters, logging_helpers, and logging_progress back into logging_utils.py (or a single logging/ subpackage) to reduce navigation cost; the 434-LOC total is well within single-file range
